trusted_domains.rationale: The implementation route /reference is registered and the UI label Admin is already used in navigation.
Current design direction remains read-only, navigational, and operator-first.
Billing detail links remain present and appear to support valid supporting breakdowns.
Product breakdown links remain in the Admin-labelled page and appear intentionally exposed.
Access drilldowns remain available and align with operator/admin review needs.

soft_scoped_domains.reason: Visibility may depend on enrichment/trust restoration and should not be oversold in Admin until verified.
License-related metrics should be presented with caution until enrichment confidence is formally restored.

soft_scoped_domains.domain: Project summary as an Admin-native surface
soft_scoped_domains.reason: Project summary currently exists as a legacy detail drilldown and should be reviewed before redesign.

operator_rules: Admin remains read-only and navigation-led during the stabilisation phase.
Operator wording should prioritise clarity over technical terminology.
Any metric shown with incomplete trust should explicitly communicate caution or withheld scope.
Admin should remain consistent with Home and Estate routing expectations.

blueprint_phases.objective: Freeze /reference as the implementation route while Admin remains the operator label.
outputs: Document trusted vs soft-scoped Admin domains
Keep legacy detail drilldowns only where they remain useful
Avoid route rename during current stabilisation window

blueprint_phases.phase: Phase B â€” Admin-native surface planning
blueprint_phases.objective: Define which legacy detail views deserve first-class Admin panels.
outputs: Decide whether project summary becomes Admin-native

blueprint_phases.objective: Only elevate soft-scoped metrics once enrichment and source confidence are proven.
outputs: Formal trust criteria for user/account/licence metrics
Operator wording guidance for caution states

blueprint_phases.phase: Phase D â€” Route modernization later
blueprint_phases.objective: Only consider /reference to /admin route changes once the Admin-native page model is mature.
outputs: Nav/home wording check after route migration

immediate_guidance: Keep /reference as the implementation route for now. The UI is already successfully presenting it as Admin.
Do not remove the detail route yet. Reference/Admin still relies on legacy detail drilldowns that appear intentionally useful.
Treat billing, product, and access detail links as acceptable transitional drilldowns until a dedicated Admin-native surface is built.
Treat project summary as the first likely candidate for a future Admin-native replacement rather than immediate removal.
Do not broaden user/licence/admin trust claims until enrichment confidence is explicitly verified.
